body:
Good morning, Alexandria friends!

Happy Monday!

To explain where I am right now, I keep looking at “Monday” in the prev sentence, there’s no red squiggle beneath it, but it still keeps looking like it’s spelled wrong.

I am, juggling a lot right now, tbh. So like 2.5 - 3 weeks ago, my partner’s dad who was diagnosed with cancer in Dec of 24 decided that the house they had lived in for 40 years was too much for him and my partner’s disabled brother and wanted to get a more manageable place. We spent the week two weeks ago, getting the place and my partner and I set it up, so they moved in on a Friday and by Saturday, they wanted to go home. And by last Monday they were.

Since we were already exhausted from the move in, we were going to take last week off and get things ready this past weekend to go home today. Tuesday we got the news that my FFIL (future father in law) has 6 - 8 weeks left. Then Thursday he fell. We rushed him to the hospital, apparently in the fall he popped his artificial hip out of socket. So while my fiancée was at the hospital with him, a friend of ours helped me out and the two of us got hi
reblog_comment:
Good morning, Alexandria friends!

Happy Monday!

To explain where I am right now, I keep looking at “Monday” in the prev sentence, there’s no red squiggle beneath it, but it still keeps looking like it’s spelled wrong.

I am, juggling a lot right now, tbh. So like 2.5 - 3 weeks ago, my partner’s dad who was diagnosed with cancer in Dec of 24 decided that the house they had lived in for 40 years was too much for him and my partner’s disabled brother and wanted to get a more manageable place. We spent the week two weeks ago, getting the place and my partner and I set it up, so they moved in on a Friday and by Saturday, they wanted to go home. And by last Monday they were.

Since we were already exhausted from the move in, we were going to take last week off and get things ready this past weekend to go home today. Tuesday we got the news that my FFIL (future father in law) has 6 - 8 weeks left. Then Thursday he fell. We rushed him to the hospital, apparently in the fall he popped his artificial hip out of socket. So while my fiancée was at the hospital with him, a friend of ours helped me out and the two of us got his bed and recliner back from the apartment and set up for him in case he came home that night.

This past weekend we did get things together to move and my fiancée is currently there with the movers. But the last 2 - 3 weeks has been a series of late nights and physical exertion and stress and…yeah. We are two emotionally and physically exhausted beans.

I’m around. I’m reacting and adding stuff to my queue. Maybe a little less OG content until things settle down a bit. But I hope life is being a bit easier on ya’ll than it is me right now. 🖤
